title: podman-logs fails with --log-driver
body:
### Issue Description

`podman logs` fails when using `--log-driver`.  The `json-file` & `k8s-file` drivers seem to be affected.

We found this issue thanks to the upstream e2e tests in openQA when testing the new conmon v2.2.0 update.  The `podman logs partial log lines` test fails both as root & rootless, with both crun & runc:

- runc root: http://openqa-assets.opensuse.org/tests/5646628/file/podman_e2e-localintegration.txt
- crun root: http://openqa-assets.opensuse.org/tests/5646627/file/podman_e2e-localintegration.txt
- runc rootless: http://openqa-assets.opensuse.org/tests/5646626/file/podman_e2e-localintegration.txt
- crun rootless: http://openqa-assets.opensuse.org/tests/5646608/file/podman_e2e-localintegration.txt

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run --log-driver json-file --name test quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest printf hola`
2. `podman logs test`



### Describe the results you received

`holaERRO[0000] Getting new log line: '2026-02-03T11:48:54.201516484+01:00 stdout F' is not a valid container log line `

Exit status: 0


### Describe the results you expected

A simple `hola`

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
This sounds like a regression from conmon v2.2.0 then.

> The json-file & k8s-file drivers seem to be affected.

json-file does not really exists and is just an alias to k8s-file which is defined by kubernetes AFAIK, so we would need to check what the actual spec says about this log line

`2026-02-03T11:48:54.201516484+01:00 stdout F` is not considered a valid line because it has no content, it is missing a space at the end.


I guess the easiest would be to hold off the conmon update and revert whatever changed this there.
cc @jnovy @lsm5 @jankaluza

k8s cannot parse such line either AFAICS, https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/bfafa32d90958a8fe7a2ce09ed553fdfef4edd98/staging/src/k8s.io/cri-client/pkg/logs/logs.go#L131-L176

so this must be fixed on the conmon end for sure
For the future and FYI: The conmon-v3 github CI will run subset of podman tests which will find out issues like that on PR level. There's a card about this I'm going to work on next sprint.
